title: Conditions on reference date
text: Unless otherwise provided in this Chapter, an issuer making an initial public offer of specified securities shall satisfy the conditions of this Chapter as on the date of filing of the draft offer document with the Board and also as on the date of filing the offer document with the Registrar of Companies.

source.span_hint: Unless otherwise provided in this Chapter

title: Disqualification: issuer/promoters/directors/sellers debarred
text: An issuer shall not be eligible to make an initial public offer if the issuer, any of its promoters, promoter group or directors or selling shareholders are debarred from accessing the capital market by the Board.

source.span_hint: if the issuer, any of its promoters

title: Disqualification: promoter/director of another debarred company
text: An issuer shall not be eligible to make an initial public offer if any of the promoters or directors of the issuer is a promoter or director of any other company which is debarred from accessing the capital market by the Board.

source.span_hint: if any of the promoters or directors

title: Disqualification: wilful defaulter or fraudulent borrower
text: An issuer shall not be eligible to make an initial public offer if the issuer or any of its promoters or directors is a wilful defaulter or a fraudulent borrower.

source.span_hint: if the issuer or any of its promoters

title: Disqualification: fugitive economic offender
text: An issuer shall not be eligible to make an initial public offer if any of its promoters or directors is a fugitive economic offender.

source.span_hint: if any of its promoters or directors

title: No outstanding convertibles/rights (pre-IPO)
text: An issuer shall not be eligible to make an initial public offer if there are any outstanding convertible securities or any other right which would entitle any person with any option to receive equity shares of the issuer.

source.span_hint: An issuer shall not be eligible

title: Net tangible assets ≥ ₹3 cr in each of last 3 years
text: it has net tangible assets of at least three crore rupees, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis, in each of the preceding three full years (of twelve months each), of which not more than fifty per cent. are held in monetary assets: Provided that if more than fifty per cent. of the net tangible assets are held in monetary assets, the issuer has utilised or made firm commitments to utilise such excess monetary assets in its business or project; Provided further that the limit of fifty per cent. on monetary assets shall not be applicable in case the initial public offer is made entirely through an offer for sale.

source.span_hint: it has net tangible assets of at least three crore rupees

title: Operating profit ≥ ₹15 cr in each of last 3 years
text: An issuer shall be eligible to make an initial public offer only if it has an average operating profit of at least fifteen crore rupees, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis, during the preceding three years (of twelve months each), with operating profit in each of these preceding three years.

source.span_hint: it has an average operating profit of at least

title: Net worth ≥ ₹1 cr in each of last 3 years
text: An issuer shall be eligible to make an initial public offer only if it has a net worth of at least one crore rupees in each of the preceding three full years (of twelve months each), calculated on a restated and consolidated basis.

source.span_hint: it has a net worth of at least

title: Outstanding options for employees (ESOP)
text: outstanding options granted to employees, whether currently an employee or not, pursuant to an employee stock option scheme in compliance with the Companies Act, 2013, the relevant Guidance Note or accounting standards, if any, issued by the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India or pursuant to the Companies Act, 2013, in this regard;

source.span_hint: outstanding options granted to employees

title: Outstanding stock appreciation rights (SAR)
text: outstanding stock appreciation rights granted to employees pursuant to a stock appreciation right scheme, which are fully exercised for equity shares prior to the filing of the red herring prospectus (in case of book-built issues) or the prospectus (in case of fixed price issues), as the case may be, disclosures regarding such stock appreciation rights and the scheme and the total number of equity shares resulting from the exercise of such rights are made in the draft offer document and offer document.

source.span_hint: outstanding stock appreciation rights granted

title: Fully paid-up convertible securities
text: fully paid-up outstanding convertible securities which are required to be converted on or before the date of filing of the red herring prospectus (in case of book-built issues) or the prospectus (in case of fixed price issues), as the case may be.

source.span_hint: fully paid-up outstanding convertible securities

title: Revenue from new name activity ≥ 50% in last year
text: if it has changed its name within the last one year, at least fifty per cent. of the revenue, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis, for the preceding one full year has been earned by it from the activity indicated by its new name.

source.span_hint: if it has changed its name within the last one year

title: Eligibility for IPO through book-building process
text: An issuer not satisfying the condition stipulated in sub-regulation (1) shall be eligible to make an initial public offer only if the issue is made through the book-building process and the issuer undertakes to allot at least seventy five per cent. of the net offer to qualified institutional buyers and to refund the full subscription money if it fails to do so.

source.span_hint: An issuer not satisfying the condition stipulated

title: General corporate purposes limit ≤ 35%
text: The amount for general corporate purposes, as mentioned in objects of the issue in the draft offer document and the offer document, shall not exceed thirty five per cent. of the amount being raised by the issuer.

source.span_hint: The amount for general corporate purposes

title: Unidentified acquisition/investment limit ≤ 25%
text: The amount raised for such objects where the issuer company has not identified acquisition or investment target, as mentioned in objects of the issue in the draft offer document and the offer document, shall not exceed twenty five per cent. of the amount being raised by the issuer.

source.span_hint: The amount raised for such objects

title: SR shares issued to promoters/founders with executive positions
text: The SR shares were issued only to the promoters/ founders who hold an executive position in the issuer company;

source.span_hint: The SR shares were issued only to the promoters

title: Authorization of SR equity shares by special resolution
text: The issue of SR equity shares had been authorized by a special resolution passed at a general meeting of the shareholders of the issuer, where the notice calling for such general meeting specifically provided for - the size of issue of SR equity shares, ratio of voting rights of SR equity shares vis-à-vis the ordinary shares, rights as to differential dividends, if any sunset provisions, which provide for a time frame for the validity of such SR equity shares, matters in respect of which the SR equity shares would have the same voting right as that of the ordinary shares.

source.span_hint: The issue of SR equity shares had been authorized

title: SR equity shares issued prior to filing draft red herring prospectus
text: the SR equity shares have been issued prior to the filing of draft red herring prospectus and held for a period of at least three months prior to the filing of the red herring prospectus;

source.span_hint: the SR equity shares have been issued prior

title: Voting rights ratio for SR equity shares
text: The SR equity shares shall have voting rights in the ratio of a minimum of 2:1 upto a maximum of 10:1 compared to ordinary shares and such ratio shall be in whole numbers only;

source.span_hint: The SR equity shares shall have voting rights

title: SR equity shares equivalent to ordinary shares except for voting rights
text: The SR equity shares shall be equivalent to ordinary equity shares in all respects, except for having superior voting rights.

source.span_hint: The SR equity shares shall be equivalent to ordinary

title: Application for in-principle approval to stock exchanges
text: it has made an application to one or more stock exchanges to seek an in-principle approval for listing of its specified securities on such stock exchanges and has chosen one of them as the designated stock exchange, in terms of Schedule XIX;

source.span_hint: made an application to one or more stock exchanges

title: Agreement with depository for dematerialisation
text: it has entered into an agreement with a depository for dematerialisation of the specified securities already issued and proposed to be issued;

source.span_hint: entered into an agreement with a depository

title: Promoters' securities in dematerialised form
text: all its specified securities held by the promoters are in dematerialised form prior to filing of the offer document;

source.span_hint: specified securities held by the promoters are in dematerialised form

title: Partly paid-up equity shares fully paid-up or forfeited
text: all its existing partly paid-up equity shares have either been fully paid-up or have been forfeited;

source.span_hint: existing partly paid-up equity shares have either been fully paid-up

title: Firm arrangements for finance towards project funding
text: it has made firm arrangements of finance through verifiable means towards seventy five per cent. of the stated means of finance for a specific project proposed to be funded from the issue proceeds, excluding the amount to be raised through the proposed public issue or through existing identifiable internal accruals.

source.span_hint: made firm arrangements of finance through verifiable means

title: Amount for general corporate purposes ≤ 25%
text: The amount for general corporate purposes, as mentioned in objects of the issue in the draft offer document and the offer document shall not exceed twenty five per cent. of the amount being raised by the issuer.

source.span_hint: amount for general corporate purposes, as mentioned in objects of the issue

title: Holding period for equity shares offered for sale
text: Only such fully paid-up equity shares may be offered for sale to the public, which have been held by the sellers for a period of at least one year prior to the filing of the draft offer document.

source.span_hint: Only such fully paid-up equity shares may be offered for sale

title: Holding period for converted securities
text: Provided that in case the equity shares received on conversion or exchange of fully paid-up compulsorily convertible securities including depository receipts are being offered for sale, the holding period of such convertible securities, including depository receipts, as well as that of resultant equity shares together shall be considered for the purpose of calculation of one year period referred in this sub-regulation.

source.span_hint: Provided that in case the equity shares received on conversion

title: Offer for sale of government company/statutory authority
text: in case of an offer for sale of a government company or statutory authority or corporation or any special purpose vehicle set up and controlled by any one or more of them, which is engaged in the infrastructure sector;

source.span_hint: in case of an offer for sale of a government company

title: Equity shares acquired pursuant to High Court/tribunal/Central Government scheme
text: if the equity shares offered for sale were acquired pursuant to any scheme approved by a High Court or approved by a tribunal or the Central Government under the sections 230 to 234 of Companies Act, 2013, as applicable, in lieu of business and invested capital which had been in existence for a period of more than one year prior to approval of such scheme;

source.span_hint: if the equity shares offered for sale were acquired

title: Equity shares issued under bonus issue
text: if the equity shares offered for sale were issued under a bonus issue on securities held for a period of at least one year prior to the filing of the draft offer document with the Board and further subject to the following:

source.span_hint: if the equity shares offered for sale were issued under a bonus issue

title: Debenture trustee requirement for convertible debt instruments
text: it has appointed at least one debenture trustee in accordance with the provisions of the Companies Act, 2013 and the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Debenture Trustees) Regulations, 1993;

source.span_hint: it has appointed at least one debenture trustee

title: Debenture redemption reserve requirement for convertible debt instruments
text: it shall create a debenture redemption reserve in accordance with the provisions of the Companies Act, 2013 and rules made thereunder;

source.span_hint: it shall create a debenture redemption reserve

title: Security on assets for convertible debt instruments
text: if the issuer proposes to create a charge or security on its assets in respect of secured convertible debt instruments, it shall ensure that:

source.span_hint: if the issuer proposes to create a charge or security

title: Positive consent for conversion of optionally convertible debt instruments
text: The issuer shall not convert its optionally convertible debt instruments into equity shares unless the holders of such convertible debt instruments have sent their positive consent to the issuer and non-receipt of reply to any notice sent by the issuer for this purpose shall not be construed as consent for conversion of any convertible debt instruments.

source.span_hint: The issuer shall not convert its optionally convertible debt instruments

title: Option to holders for conversion of listed convertible debt instruments
text: Where the value of the convertible portion of any listed convertible debt instruments issued by an issuer exceeds ten crore rupees and the issuer has not determined the conversion price of such convertible debt instruments at the time of making the issue, the holders of such convertible debt instruments shall be given the option of not converting the convertible portion into equity shares: Provided that where the upper limit on the price of such convertible debt instruments and justification thereon is determined and disclosed to the investors at the time of making the issue, it shall not be necessary to give such option to the holders of the convertible debt instruments for converting the convertible portion into equity share capital within the said upper limit.

source.span_hint: Where the value of the convertible portion of any listed

title: Redemption within one month for non-exercised conversion option
text: Where an option is to be given to the holders of the convertible debt instruments in terms of sub-regulation (2) and if one or more of such holders do not exercise the option to convert the instruments into equity share capital at a price determined in the general meeting of the shareholders, the issuer shall redeem that part of the instruments within one month from the last date by which option is to be exercised, at a price which shall not be less than its face value.

source.span_hint: Where an option is to be given to the holders of

title: Redemption as per disclosures in offer document
text: The provision of sub-regulation (2) shall not apply if such redemption is as per the disclosures made in the offer document.

source.span_hint: The provision of sub-regulation (2) shall not apply

title: Restriction on issuing convertible debt instruments for financing or loans to promoter group
text: An issuer shall not issue convertible debt instruments for financing or for providing loans to or for acquiring shares of any person who is part of the promoter group or group companies: Provided that an issuer shall be eligible to issue fully convertible debt instruments for these purposes if the period of conversion of such debt instruments is less than eighteen months from the date of issue of such debt instruments.

source.span_hint: An issuer shall not issue convertible debt instruments

title: Issue of warrants in initial public offer
text: An issuer shall be eligible to issue warrants in an initial public offer subject to the following: a) the tenure of such warrants shall not exceed eighteen months from the date of their allotment; b) a specified security may have one or more warrants attached to it; c) the price or formula for determination of exercise price of the warrants shall be determined upfront and disclosed in the offer document and at least twenty-five per cent. of the consideration amount based on the exercise price shall also be received upfront: Provided that in case the exercise price of warrants is based on a formula, twenty-five per cent. consideration amount based on the cap price of the price band determined for the linked equity shares or convertible securities shall be received upfront; d) in case the warrant holder does not exercise the option to take equity shares against any of the warrants held by the warrant holder, within three months from the date of payment of consideration, such consideration made in respect of such warrants shall be forfeited by the issuer.

source.span_hint: An issuer shall be eligible to issue warrants

title: Promoters' contribution ≥ 20% post-issue capital
text: The promoters of the issuer shall hold at least twenty per cent. of the post-issue capital: Provided that in case the post-issue shareholding of the promoters is less than twenty per cent., alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India 37[or any non-individual public shareholder holding at least five per cent. of the post-issue capital or any entity (individual or non-individual) forming part of promoter group other than the promoter(s)] may contribute to meet the shortfall in minimum contribution as specified for the promoters, subject to a maximum of ten per cent. of the post-issue capital without being identified as promoter(s). Provided further that the requirement of minimum promoters’ contribution shall not apply in case an issuer does not have any identifiable promoter.

source.span_hint: The promoters of the issuer shall hold at least

title: Warrant tenure ≤ 18 months
text: the tenure of such warrants shall not exceed eighteen months from the date of their allotment in the initial public offer;

source.span_hint: the tenure of such warrants shall not exceed

title: Exercise price upfront and 25% consideration received
text: the price or formula for determination of exercise price of the warrants shall be determined upfront and disclosed in the offer document and at least twenty-five per cent. of the consideration amount based on the exercise price shall also be received upfront;

source.span_hint: the price or formula for determination of exercise

title: Forfeiture of consideration if not exercised within 3 months
text: in case the warrant holder does not exercise the option to take equity shares against any of the warrants held by the warrant holder, within three months from the date of payment of consideration, such consideration made in respect of such warrants shall be forfeited by the issuer.

source.span_hint: in case the warrant holder does not exercise

title: Promoters' contribution by equity shares or convertible securities
text: the promoters shall contribute twenty per cent. as stipulated in sub-regulation (1), as the case may be, either by way of equity shares38[, including SR equity shares held, if any,] or by way of subscription to convertible securities: Provided that if the price of the equity shares allotted pursuant to conversion is not pre-determined and not disclosed in the offer document, the promoters shall contribute only by way of subscription to the convertible securities being issued in the public issue and shall undertake in writing to subscribe to the equity shares pursuant to conversion of such securities.

source.span_hint: the promoters shall contribute twenty per cent.

title: Price of equity shares not lower than weighted average price
text: in case of any issue of convertible securities which are convertible or exchangeable on different dates and if the promoters’ contribution is by way of equity shares (conversion price being pre-determined), such contribution shall not be at a price lower than the weighted average price of the equity share capital arising out of conversion of such securities.

source.span_hint: in case of any issue of convertible securities which are

title: Promoters' contribution for convertible debt instruments
text: subject to the provisions of clause (a) and (b) above, in case of an initial public offer of convertible debt instruments without a prior public issue of equity shares, the promoters shall bring in a contribution of at least twenty per cent. of the project cost in the form of equity shares, subject to contributing at least twenty per cent. of the issue size from their own funds in the form of equity shares: Provided that if the project is to be implemented in stages, the promoters’ contribution shall be with respect to total equity participation till the respective stage vis-à-vis the debt raised or proposed to be raised through the public issue.

source.span_hint: subject to the provisions of clause (a) and

title: Promoters' contribution requirements satisfied one day prior to issue opening
text: The promoters shall satisfy the requirements of this regulation at least one day prior to the date of opening of the issue.

source.span_hint: The promoters shall satisfy the requirements of this

title: Promoters' contribution escrow account and release conditions
text: In case the promoters have to subscribe to equity shares or convertible securities towards minimum promoters’ contribution, the amount of promoters’ contribution shall be kept in an escrow account with a scheduled commercial bank, which shall be released to the issuer along with the release of the issue proceeds: Provided that where the promoters’ contribution has already been brought in and utilised, the issuer shall give the cash flow statement disclosing the use of such funds in the offer document; Provided further that where the minimum promoters’ contribution is more than one hundred crore rupees and the initial public offer is for partly paid shares, the promoters shall bring in at least one hundred crore rupees before the date of opening of the issue and the remaining amount may be brought on a pro-rata basis before the calls are made to the public.

source.span_hint: In case the promoters have to subscribe to equity

title: Specified securities ineligible for minimum promoters’ contribution (a)
text: specified securities acquired during the preceding three years, if these are: (i) acquired for consideration other than cash and revaluation of assets or capitalisation of intangible assets is involved in such transaction; or (ii) resulting from a bonus issue by utilisation of revaluation reserves or unrealised profits of the issuer or from bonus issue against equity shares which are ineligible for minimum promoters’ contribution;

source.span_hint: specified securities acquired during the preceding three years

title: Specified securities ineligible for minimum promoters’ contribution (b)
text: specified securities acquired by the promoters and alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India or any non-individual public shareholder holding at least five per cent. of the post-issue capital or any entity (individual or non-individual) forming part of promoter group other than the promoter(s), during the preceding one year at a price lower than the price at which specified securities are being offered to the public in the initial public offer:

source.span_hint: specified securities acquired by the promoters and alternative investment funds

title: Specified securities ineligible for minimum promoters’ contribution
text: (c) specified securities allotted to the promoters and alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India or any non-individual public shareholder holding at least five per cent. of the post-issue capital or any entity (individual or non-individual) forming part of promoter group other than the promoter(s) during the preceding one year at a price less than the issue price, against funds brought in by them during that period, in case of an issuer formed by conversion of one or more partnership firms or limited liability partnerships, where the partners of the erstwhile partnership firms or limited liability partnerships are the promoters of the issuer and there is no change in the management: Provided that specified securities, allotted to the promoters against the capital existing in such firms for a period of more than one year on a continuous basis, shall be eligible;
notes: Map to promoter_contribution and entity_types (promoters, AIFs, FVCI, banks, PFIs, insurance companies).

source.span_hint: specified securities allotted to the promoters and alternative investment funds

title: Eligibility of specified securities for promoters’ contribution under clauses (a) and (c)
text: (2) Specified securities referred to in clauses (a) and (c) of sub-regulation (1) shall be eligible for the computation of promoters' contribution if such securities are acquired pursuant to a scheme which has been approved by a High Court or tribunal or the Central Government under sections 230 to 234 of the Companies Act, 2013.

source.span_hint: Specified securities referred to in clauses (a) and (c)

title: Lock-in period for minimum promoters’ contribution
text: (a) minimum promoters' contribution including contribution made by alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India or any non-individual public shareholder holding at least five per cent. of the post-issue capital or any entity (individual or non-individual) forming part of promoter group other than the promoter(s) referred to in proviso to sub-regulation (1) of regulation 14, shall be locked-in for a period of eighteen months from the date of allotment in the initial public offer: Provided that in case the majority of the issue proceeds excluding the portion of offer for sale is proposed to be utilized for capital expenditure, then the lock-in period shall be three years from the date of allotment in the initial public offer.

source.span_hint: minimum promoters' contribution including contribution made by alternative investment funds

title: Lock-in period for excess promoters’ holding
text: (b) promoters' holding in excess of minimum promoters' contribution shall be locked-in for a period of six months from the date of allotment in the initial public offer. Provided that in case the majority of the issue proceeds excluding the portion of offer for sale is proposed to be utilized for capital expenditure, then the lock-in period shall be one year from the date of allotment in the initial public offer.

source.span_hint: promoters' holding in excess of minimum promoters' contribution

title: Lock-in for SR equity shares
text: The SR equity shares shall be under lock-in until conversion into equity shares having voting rights same as that of ordinary shares or shall be locked-in for a period specified in sub-regulations (1), whichever is later.
notes: Map to sr_equity_shares_lock_in_period (whichever is later: conversion date or 18/3 years).

source.span_hint: The SR equity shares shall be under lock-in

title: Pre-issue capital lock-in for non-promoters
text: The entire pre-issue capital held by persons other than the promoters shall be locked-in for a period of six months from the date of allotment in the initial public offer.

source.span_hint: The entire pre-issue capital held by persons other than the promoters

title: Exemption for employee stock options/schemes
text: Nothing contained in this regulation shall apply to equity shares allotted to employees, whether currently an employee or not, under an employee stock option or employee stock purchase scheme or a stock appreciation right scheme of the issuer prior to the initial public offer, if the issuer has made full disclosures with respect to such options or scheme in accordance with Part A of Schedule VI.

source.span_hint: equity shares allotted to employees, whether currently an employee or not

title: Exemption for employee stock option trust
text: Nothing contained in this regulation shall apply to equity shares held by an employee stock option trust or transferred to the employees by an employee stock option trust pursuant to exercise of options by the employees, whether currently employees or not, in accordance with the employee stock option plan or employee stock purchase scheme or a stock appreciation rights scheme.

source.span_hint: equity shares held by an employee stock option trust or transferred to the employees

title: Exemption for venture capital funds/alternative investment funds
text: Nothing contained in this regulation shall apply to equity shares held by a venture capital fund or alternative investment fund of category I or Category II or a foreign venture capital investor, provided that such equity shares shall be locked-in for a period of at least six months from the date of purchase by the venture capital fund or alternative investment fund of Category I or Category II or foreign venture capital investor.

source.span_hint: equity shares held by a venture capital fund or alternative investment fund

title: Lock-in provisions for specified securities lent to stabilising agent under green shoe option
text: The lock-in provisions shall not apply with respect to the specified securities lent to stabilising agent for the purpose of green shoe option, during the period starting from the date of lending of such specified securities and ending on the date on which they are returned to the lender in terms of sub-regulation (5) or (6) of regulation 57: Provided that the specified securities shall be locked-in for the remaining period from the date on which they are returned to the lender.

source.span_hint: The lock-in provisions shall not apply with respect to the specified securities lent

title: Lock-in for partly paid-up specified securities
text: If the specified securities which are subject to lock-in are partly paid-up and the amount called-up on such specified securities is less than the amount called-up on the specified securities issued to the public, the lock-in shall end only on the expiry of three years after such specified securities have become pari passu with the specified securities issued to the public.

source.span_hint: If the specified securities which are subject to lock-in are partly paid-up

title: Inscription or recording of non-transferability for locked-in specified securities
text: The certificates of specified securities which are subject to lock-in shall contain the inscription “non-transferable” and specify the lock-in period and in case such specified securities are dematerialised, the issuer shall ensure that the lock-in is recorded by the depository.

source.span_hint: The certificates of specified securities which are subject to lock-in shall contain

title: Pledge of locked-in specified securities by promoters
text: Specified securities, except SR equity shares, held by the promoters and locked-in may be pledged as a collateral security for a loan granted by a scheduled commercial bank or a public financial institution or a systemically important non-banking finance company or a housing finance company, subject to the following: if the specified securities are locked-in in terms of clause (a) of regulation 16, the loan has been granted to the issuer company or its subsidiary(ies) for the purpose of financing one or more of the objects of the issue and pledge of specified securities is one of the terms of sanction of the loan; if the specified securities are locked-in in terms of clause (b) of regulation 16 and the pledge of specified securities is one of the terms of sanction of the loan. Provided that such lock-in shall continue pursuant to the invocation of the pledge and such transferee shall not be eligible to transfer the specified securities till the lock-in period stipulated in these regulations has expired.

source.span_hint: Specified securities, except SR equity shares, held by the promoters and locked-in

title: Pledge conditions for clause (a) of regulation 16
text: if the specified securities are locked-in in terms of clause (a) of regulation 16, the loan has been granted to the issuer company or its subsidiary(ies) for the purpose of financing one or more of the objects of the issue and pledge of specified securities is one of the terms of sanction of the loan;

source.span_hint: if the specified securities are locked-in in terms of clause (a)

title: Pledge conditions for clause (b) of regulation 16
text: if the specified securities are locked-in in terms of clause (b) of regulation 16 and the pledge of specified securities is one of the terms of sanction of the loan.

source.span_hint: if the specified securities are locked-in in terms of clause (b)

title: Transferability of locked-in specified securities
text: Subject to the provisions of Securities and Exchange Board of India (Substantial Acquisition of shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011, the specified securities[, except SR equity shares,] held by the promoters and locked-in as per regulation 16, may be transferred to another promoter or any person of the promoter group or a new promoter and the specified securities held by persons other than the promoters and locked-in as per regulation 17, may be transferred to any other person holding the specified securities which are locked-in along with the securities proposed to be transferred: Provided that the lock-in on such specified securities shall continue for the remaining period with the transferee and such transferee shall not be eligible to transfer them till the lock-in period stipulated in these regulations has expired.

source.span_hint: Subject to the provisions of Securities and Exchange Board

title: Appointment of lead managers
text: The issuer shall appoint one or more merchant bankers, which are registered with the Board, as lead manager(s) to the issue.

source.span_hint: The issuer shall appoint one or more merchant bankers

title: Rights, obligations and responsibilities of lead managers
text: Where the issue is managed by more than one lead manager, the rights, obligations and responsibilities, relating inter alia to disclosures, allotment, refund and underwriting obligations, if any, of each lead manager shall be predetermined and be disclosed in the draft offer document and the offer document as specified in Schedule I.

source.span_hint: Where the issue is managed by more than one lead manager

title: Non-associate lead manager requirement
text: At least one lead manager to the issue shall not be an associate (as defined under the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Merchant Bankers) Regulations, 1992) of the issuer and if any of the lead manager is an associate of the issuer, it shall disclose itself as an associate of the issuer and its role shall be limited to marketing of the issue.

source.span_hint: At least one lead manager to the issue shall not be an associate

title: Appointment of other intermediaries
text: The issuer shall, in consultation with the lead manager(s), appoint other intermediaries which are registered with the Board after the lead manager(s) have independently assessed the capability of other intermediaries to carry out their obligations.

source.span_hint: The issuer shall, in consultation with the lead manager(s)

title: Agreements with Intermediaries
text: The issuer shall enter into an agreement with the lead manager(s) in the format specified in Schedule II and enter into agreements with other intermediaries as required under the respective regulations applicable to the intermediary concerned: Provided that such agreements may include such other clauses as the issuer and the intermediaries may deem fit without diminishing or limiting in any way the liabilities and obligations of the lead manager.
notes: Ensure agreements are in specified format and do not diminish liabilities/obligations.

source.span_hint: The issuer shall enter into an agreement with the lead manager(s)
